name: Bad Beans
classification_notes: Bad Beans is a casual, marker-style handwritten script designed to mimic informal scribbled lettering with intentional imperfections, contextual alternates and uneven strokes.
summary: A marker-style handwritten script that leans into imperfection, with contextual alternates cycling through letter variations to avoid repetition.
description: Built around the aesthetic of scribbled notes and skatepark doodles, Bad Beans mimics the uneven lines and awkward charm of informal marker lettering. Two sets of contextual alternates automatically rotate through character variations as you type, preventing obvious repeated glyphs and maintaining a natural handwritten rhythm. The 484-glyph set includes multilingual support for over 100 languages and 29 sketched illustrations accessible via keyboard shortcuts.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Bad Beans as a messy throwback to informal marker lettering, inspired by scribbled notes, old CDs covered in handwriting and skatepark doodles. They frame it as a deliberate rejection of over-designed polish, with uneven lines and wonky shapes treated as features rather than flaws. The font includes contextual alternates that cycle through letter variations automatically, 484 glyphs, support for over 100 languages and 29 matching sketch illustrations.

name: Trail Notes
classification_notes: Both fonts in the duo are casual handwritten scripts with an organic, textured finish, placing them firmly in the handwritten script subcategory.
summary: A handwritten font duo pairing the rugged, vintage-inflected Wild Pines Park with the friendlier, annotation-ready Naturalist, sold as a matched bundle.
description: Trail Notes bundles two handwritten scripts designed to work together out of the box. Wild Pines Park carries a nostalgic, field-guide quality suited to titles and branding, while Naturalist offers a lighter, textured hand more at home in labels, annotations and small-text moments. Both have been updated with multilingual character sets and improved kerning, and the bundle includes ligatures and a range of glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Trail Notes as a handwritten font duo made for people who like their type a little wild around the edges. Wild Pines Park brings national park nostalgia and a rugged vintage feel, while Naturalist is a simple, charming handwritten notes font with a warm, realistic finish. The two are designed to pair together naturally, and the bundle includes multilingual support, ligatures and free updates.

name: Notemaker's Mark
classification_notes: Informal, forward-leaning letterforms modelled on spontaneous handwritten notes and scribbles; sits clearly in the casual handwriting script subcategory rather than formal calligraphy or brush script.
summary: A casual handwriting script modelled on spontaneous notebook scribbles, with forward-leaning letterforms designed to feel energetic and approachable.
description: Built around the aesthetic of notes written in the moment rather than polished penmanship, the typeface carries natural inconsistencies and a slight forward lean that give it a sense of movement. It suits branding, packaging, social content and editorial work where an informal human touch is needed without sacrificing legibility. The relaxed character makes it a practical choice for lifestyle brands and creative projects that want personality over precision.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Notemaker's Mark as handwriting for ideas in motion, inspired by meeting notes, whiteboard scrawls and sticky-note thinking rather than perfect penmanship. They position it as loose, expressive and full of character, suited to branding, packaging, social content and editorial design where an approachable, authentic and spontaneous quality is required.

name: Mrs Pickles
classification_notes: A hand-drawn marker-style font with rounded edges and playful character, placing it firmly in the informal script category with a casual, brush-marker subcategory.
summary: A rounded, hand-drawn marker font with three alternate character sets for A–Z, a–z and 0–9, aimed at playful branding and packaging.
description: Mrs Pickles is a marker-style script with rounded edges and an informal, hand-drawn character suited to children's products, pet stores and feel-good packaging. Three unique character sets for uppercase, lowercase and numerals give designers variety within a single weight. Multi-lingual support extends its reach beyond English-language projects.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Mrs Pickles as a friendly marker font with rounded edges and playful charm that brings hand-drawn warmth to any design. They position it for kids' brands, feel-good quotes, cute packaging and social posts, noting its three unique character sets for A–Z, a–z and 0–9 alongside multi-lingual support.

name: Naturalist
classification_notes: A casual handwritten script mimicking informal handwritten notes, with ligatures and texture that reinforce its script classification rather than display lettering.
summary: A casual handwritten font with ligatures and subtle texture, aimed at annotations, labels and warm informal copy.
description: Naturalist mimics the look of handwritten notes, with a slightly textured quality and natural-feeling ligatures for common pairs such as ee, th and st. It covers a broad range of Latin-based languages including French, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Dutch, Icelandic and several Scandinavian languages. Suited to labelling, annotation graphics and any context where a realistic handwritten feel is preferred over a polished calligraphic script.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Naturalist as a simple but charming handwritten notes font with an approachable, slightly textured vibe. They highlight its suitability for annotations, labels and warm realistic contexts, and note a significant upgrade that brought multilingual support across a wide range of Latin-based languages, as well as refined ligatures and improved texture and handwritten authenticity.

name: Wild Pines Park
classification_notes: A bold decorative display face with a retro Americana and national park aesthetic, featuring distinctive letterforms suited to headlines and branding rather than continuous text.
summary: A bold retro display face styled after American national park signage and field guide typography, with distinctive letterforms in the K, R and G.
description: Wild Pines Park draws on the visual language of mid-century American national parks, combining a commanding weight with warm, nostalgic character. Distinctive glyphs give the face personality beyond generic retro display fonts, making it useful for branding, poster work and editorial contexts that need an outdoorsy, Americana feel. Version 2.0 added multilingual character support and new symbols, broadening its practical range.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Wild Pines Park as a nostalgic, national park-inspired display font with a subtle ruggedness and Americana charm, evoking a bygone era of field guide notebooks and wilderness adventure. They highlight its commanding yet comforting presence and draw attention to playful, unique characters in the K, R and G. Version 2.0 brings multilingual support, new symbols and a range of improvements.

name: Sundara
classification_notes: A wide, textured display sans with a handmade quality and generous proportions, positioned explicitly for headlines and branding rather than body text use.
summary: A wide, lightly textured display sans with a handmade quality suited to packaging, headlines and brand identity work.
description: Built around generous letter spacing and a wide stance, it carries a subtle handmade texture that softens its sans-serif structure without tipping into script territory. The short cap height and open proportions give it an unhurried, airy rhythm well suited to social media graphics, packaging and brand headlines. The 217-glyph set includes multilingual and extended Latin characters.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Sundara as a spacious, textured display sans designed to bring warmth and character to projects. Its wide proportions, soft texture and generous letter spacing are aimed at brands valuing authenticity and elegance, with a short cap height that makes it suited to headlines, packaging, social media and branding with understated confidence.

name: Belmonte Ballpoint
classification_notes: Designed to replicate the appearance of ballpoint pen handwriting, with both print and cursive styles that mimic the texture and imperfections of ink on paper, placing it firmly in the handwritten script category.
summary: A dual-style handwriting family designed to replicate the texture and imperfection of ballpoint pen ink, available in both print and cursive cuts.
description: Two complementary styles, print and cursive, simulate the organic irregularities of writing with a ballpoint pen. The family includes three alternate sets of A-Z and 0-9 characters alongside an extended Latin character set for multilingual use. A set of 25 scribble glyphs, mapped to keyboard shortcuts, extends the family into annotation and mark-up territory.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Belmonte Ballpoint as a versatile typeface with print and cursive styles that capture the organic, slightly textured look of ballpoint pen ink, including the imperfections that make handwriting feel personal. The family offers three sets of A-Z and 0-9, extended Latin characters for multilingual use, and 25 scribble glyphs accessible via keyboard shortcuts for a realistic pen mark-up effect.

name: Halloween Handwritten Font Bundle
classification_notes: The bundle comprises hand-drawn, handwritten display scripts with a horror and Halloween aesthetic, ranging from eerie to playful in character.
summary: A bundle of eight hand-drawn Halloween-themed display fonts spanning eerie to playful styles, sold exclusively through Typeheist.
description: Eight handwritten fonts collected under a single Halloween-themed bundle, each described as hand-drawn and full of idiosyncratic character. The range runs from unsettling to monstrous to lighthearted, with glyphs and ligatures included across the set. Suited to seasonal work such as posters, event invitations, party decor and social media graphics.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe this as a bundle of scribbly, scratchy, ghoulish handwriting fonts with a range of glyphs and ligatures, designed for Halloween posters, invites, trick-or-treat bags and party decor. Eight fonts are included at up to 25% off compared to individual purchases, with free updates and exclusive availability through typeheist.co.

name: Juniper Bay
classification_notes: A marker-style casual script with organic letterforms and extensive ligature support, characteristic of informal handwritten typefaces.
summary: A casual marker script with over 135 custom ligatures and stylistic alternates, suited to handwritten notes, fashion work and wedding stationery.
description: A relaxed handwritten typeface drawn in a marker style, with an emphasis on natural rhythm and variety. Version 2.0 adds 67 new characters, 13 new custom ligatures and 42 scribble illustrations alongside improved kerning and spacing. A full stylistic alternates set offers an alternative character suite with the same overall feel.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Juniper Bay as a cool, calm and sophisticated handwritten typeface with an organic feel and effortless vibe. They highlight its 135-plus custom ligatures and suitability for handwritten notes, fashion mockups, social media and wedding stationery. Version 2.0 brings improved kerning, legibility and spacing alongside new characters, ligatures and scribble illustrations.

name: Pandemonium
classification_notes: An irregular, scratchy handwriting font with deliberately erratic letterforms that vary in width and weight, placing it firmly in the script/handwriting category rather than any formal script tradition.
summary: A deliberately chaotic handwriting font with four alternating character sets and 78 scribble glyphs, designed to feel genuinely unpredictable on the page.
description: Each letter shifts in width, weight and texture, with skinny strokes sitting next to bold, scratchy ones in a way that resists any sense of mechanical regularity. Four complete sets of A-Z and 0-9 characters rotate through to keep repetition from settling in, while 78 scribble glyphs add extra visual noise via keyboard shortcuts. Extended Latin coverage makes it usable across multiple languages despite its rough, anarchic character.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Pandemonium as wild, chaotic and super erratic, naming it after the dictionary definition of unrestrained disorder and tumult. The font refuses to follow rules, varying from skinny to wide, bold to scratchy, yet each letter somehow harmonises with the next. It ships with four sets of A-Z and 0-9, extended Latin characters and 78 scribble glyphs accessible via keyboard shortcuts.

name: Wasted Year
classification_notes: A casual, messy handwriting script with an unpredictable mix of uppercase and lowercase letterforms, placing it firmly in the informal script category.
summary: A messy handwriting script that unpredictably alternates between uppercase and lowercase letterforms depending on letter combinations entered.
description: The defining feature of this font is its contextual case-switching: rather than a fixed upper or lowercase, the letterforms change according to the surrounding characters, producing an authentically erratic handwritten feel. This makes each string of text feel genuinely spontaneous rather than mechanically repeated. Suited to informal branding, social content or any setting where a raw, unstudied hand is called for.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Wasted Year as a wonderfully wild, messy handwriting font whose key characteristic is an erratic switch between uppercase and lowercase letters. The case of each letter shifts based on the surrounding letter combinations, meaning the result is always unpredictable.

name: Peachy Font Bundle
classification_notes: The bundle consists of casual handwriting fonts described as cute and handcrafted, placing them firmly in the script/casual handwriting category.
summary: A bundle of four casual handwriting fonts from Typeheist, sold together at a discount with ligature support.
description: Four handcrafted casual handwriting fonts sold as a discounted bundle exclusive to the Typeheist website. Each font includes a range of glyphs and ligatures suited to informal, decorative use. The bundle includes free future updates and is available with desktop and web licensing options.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe the Peachy Font Bundle as four cute and casual handwriting fonts offering up to 25% off compared to individual purchases. The bundle includes unique handcrafted fonts with a range of glyphs and ligatures, free updates, and is available exclusively through typeheist.co.

name: Sticky
classification_notes: Sticky is a casual handwriting script designed to replicate the irregularities of genuine handwritten text, with alternates and ligatures reinforcing its informal, personal character.
summary: A two-weight casual handwriting font built with extensive alternates and ligatures to produce natural-feeling, varied text.
description: Designed to replicate the slight inconsistencies of real handwriting, Sticky comes in Regular and Bold weights with a broad glyph set including ligatures and alternates to avoid mechanical repetition. The letterforms are deliberately a little loose and irregular, lending projects a warm, approachable quality without tipping into formality. Suited to branding, packaging or editorial contexts where a friendly, hand-crafted feel is the goal.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Sticky as a realistic and slightly offbeat handwriting font in two weights. They note it is warm and approachable with a playful edge, packed with glyphs, ligatures and alternates to give text natural flow and variation, with every letter feeling a little different to add personality and charm. They call it a little messy, a little weird and a lot of fun, suited to projects calling for a friendly and slightly unusual touch.

name: Messy Font Bundle
classification_notes: The bundle consists of five handwriting fonts described as messy and handcrafted, placing them squarely in the informal script/handwriting category.
summary: A bundle of five informal handwriting fonts from Typeheist, sold at a discount exclusively through their own site.
description: Five messy handwriting fonts sold together at up to 25% off compared to individual purchases. Each font is described as handcrafted with a range of glyphs and ligatures for variety in use. The bundle covers desktop and web licensing and includes free updates to all included fonts.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist position the Messy Font Bundle as the best way to buy multiple fonts from their catalogue, offering five handcrafted messy handwriting fonts with a range of glyphs and ligatures at up to 25% off, available only through typeheist.co, with free updates included.

name: Prickly Pear
classification_notes: An informal, irregular handwritten script with extensive ligatures designed to mimic natural pen scrawl; falls squarely into casual handwritten script territory.
summary: A rough handwritten script scrawled in a moving car crossing the Simpson Desert, with 160-plus ligatures to keep the letterforms unpredictably varied.
description: Created on a road trip through the Australian outback, the typeface mimics the uneven quality of pen-on-paper writing done in motion. Over 160 ligatures distribute variation across the letterforms so that repeated characters avoid mechanical repetition. It covers uppercase, lowercase and multilingual Latin characters.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Prickly Pear as a messy handwritten font with a unique backstory, scribbled in a moving car somewhere in the middle of Australia on a road trip to the Simpson Desert. The result is a rugged, offbeat typeface with 160-plus ligatures, uppercase and lowercase, multilingual support and what they call just the right amount of chaos.

name: Ugly Dave
classification_notes: An informal handwriting script designed to mimic bad, childish handwriting with deliberate messiness and imperfection throughout its letterforms.
summary: A deliberately messy handwriting font with over 500 glyphs and ligatures designed to mimic convincingly bad, childish script.
description: Comes in two styles, Regular and Alternates, each with distinct letterforms to maintain the illusion of genuine handwriting. With over 500 glyphs and ligatures, the character set is broad enough to avoid repetition and keep the casual, imperfect quality consistent. Despite the deliberate messiness, it remains legible at both large and small sizes.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Ugly Dave as messy, childish and unique, with a certain charm to its imperfections. It includes over 500 glyphs and ligatures across two styles, Regular and Alternates, both described as realistic and handwritten. The foundry position it as a solution for designs that call for a touch of imperfection.

name: Infamous
classification_notes: Infamous is a casual, slightly italicised handwriting font with a rough, textured appearance and scribble ornaments, placing it firmly in the script/handwriting subcategory.
summary: A rough, slightly italicised handwriting font with a scratchy, inked texture and over 100 custom ligatures for natural flow.
description: Drawn to mimic the look of real ink on paper, Infamous carries a deliberately imperfect, distressed character suited to poster work, packaging and branding that needs a grunge or handcrafted feel. The single weight includes uppercase and lowercase, multilingual support and over 360 glyphs in total, among them hand-drawn scribble characters accessible via keyboard shortcuts. Those sketched ornaments sit naturally alongside the letterforms, adding extra personality without feeling bolted on.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Infamous as a slightly italicised messy handwriting font that is rough, scratchy and textured, intended to capture the authenticity of real ink on paper. They position it for posters, branding, packaging and digital design, highlighting its bold expressive character, over 100 custom ligatures, multilingual support and more than 360 unique glyphs including hand-drawn scribble characters.

name: Mega Font Bundle 2023
classification_notes: The bundle is described as comprising realistic handwriting fonts with handcrafted character sets and ligatures, placing it squarely in the script/handwriting category.
summary: A bundle of 17 realistic handwriting fonts from Typeheist's full 2023 catalogue, sold at a discount for desktop and web use.
description: Collects the entire Typeheist catalogue as of November 2023 into a single purchase, covering 17 handwriting-style fonts with varying glyph sets and ligature support. The bundle is sold exclusively through typeheist.co and includes free updates to the included fonts. Pricing reflects up to 25% off the combined desktop and web licence cost.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe this as the best way to buy multiple fonts from their catalogue, bundling all 17 realistic handwriting fonts released up to November 2023 with unique handcrafted glyphs and ligatures, available only through their own site with free future updates included.

name: Manic
classification_notes: Manic is a casual handwriting script mimicking pencil scrawl on paper, with multiple alternates and ligatures to simulate authentic hand-lettered messiness.
summary: A scribbly pencil-style handwriting font with four style variants, 650+ glyphs and alternates designed to simulate genuinely messy hand lettering.
description: Built to replicate the erratic quality of pencil-scrawled handwriting, the family ships in four distinct styles alongside a fifth sketches font containing scratchy lines, circles, crosses and scribbles in PNG and SVG formats. Over 650 glyphs include extensive ligatures and character alternates that can be mixed freely to avoid the mechanical repetition common in script fonts. Multi-lingual Latin support broadens its practical reach beyond English-language use.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Manic as a truly messy handwriting font that is scribbly, scratchy and unique, with over 650 glyphs and ligatures and four distinct but equally chaotic styles. They note it looks like real handwriting scrawled onto paper with a lead pencil, and include a bonus set of matching scratchy sketch elements in PNG and SVG formats.

name: Sad Poem
classification_notes: A casual handwritten pen script with irregular, organic letterforms designed to mimic authentic handwriting. Classified as script due to its handwritten character and flowing, informal construction.
summary: A skinny, irregular handwritten pen script with over 360 glyphs, custom ligatures and an extended symbol set for expressive, informal use.
description: Designed to feel spontaneous and raw, it mimics the uneven quality of genuine pen handwriting with deliberately wonky, wiggly letterforms. The character set covers both upper and lowercase, multilingual characters and custom ligatures, giving it more typographic range than most casual scripts. Best suited to personal projects, social content or packaging where an emotional, handmade quality is the point.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Sad Poem as a messy handwritten pen font with over 360 glyphs, featuring a quirky realistic charm. The font includes multilingual characters, custom ligatures and an extended symbol set, with both uppercase and lowercase letters, making it versatile yet raw.

name: Marina
classification_notes: Marina is a handwritten cursive script with ligatures, characteristic of casual script fonts designed to mimic natural handwriting with a feminine, flowing quality.
summary: A flowing cursive handwriting font with over 80 ligatures aimed at designs requiring a soft, feminine character.
description: A handwritten script face built around natural cursive letterforms with more than 80 ligatures that help create the impression of continuous, connected writing. Intended for projects where a gentle, personal quality is needed, such as wedding stationery, greeting cards or lifestyle branding. Single weight offering without italics or condensed variants.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Marina as a feminine yet modern handwritten font suitable for adding warmth to designs, with dainty cursive letterforms ideal for classy, gentle projects. They highlight its library of over 80 ligatures as the feature that elevates standard text into a beautiful handwritten script.

name: Voodoo
classification_notes: An all-caps, scratchy hand-drawn font with deliberate scribble texture, placing it firmly in the display/novelty category rather than a general script or handwriting classification.
summary: An all-caps, scratchy Halloween display font supplied with over 40 matching scribble illustrations including skulls, ghosts and voodoo dolls.
description: Voodoo is a hand-drawn, all-caps typeface built around a deliberate scratchy and scribbled texture suited to Halloween and seasonal horror themes. It comes in Regular and Bold weights and ships with a set of more than 40 matching illustrations covering skulls, spiders, ghosts and voodoo dolls. Its scope is narrow and intentional, making it a ready-made toolkit for themed packaging, event posters or seasonal branding.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Voodoo as a scratchy, scribbled all-caps Halloween font with a spooky handwriting character, available in regular and bold weights and bundled with 40+ matching scribble illustrations including voodoo dolls, spiders, ghosts and skulls.

name: Reuben
classification_notes: A bold, condensed display face drawn from hand-lettering and sign painting traditions, placing it firmly in the display category with a vernacular, retro subcategory.
summary: A bold condensed display face drawn from classic sign painting, carrying a retro, hand-lettered character suited to nostalgic or craft-oriented work.
description: Reuben takes its cues from vintage sign painting, offering bold strokes and a condensed form that suits diner menus, tattoo studios and similar nostalgic contexts. Subtle detailing in the letterforms gives it a degree of personality beyond generic retro revival territory. A single-weight display font best deployed at large sizes where its hand-crafted qualities read clearly.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Reuben as a hand-lettered, retro condensed font inspired by classic sign painting. They note its bold strokes and old-school character as fitting for diner menus, tattoo parlours and creative studios, citing subtle yet distinctive details as the source of its charm and statement quality.

name: Serial
classification_notes: An uppercase display typeface drawn from erratic handwriting, placing it firmly in the display category with a hand-lettered, irregular character that defies conventional script classification.
summary: An uppercase display face drawn from the erratic, irregular handwriting found in letters attributed to the Son of Sam serial killer.
description: Built from the chaotic penmanship of a notorious serial killer's correspondence, Serial is an all-caps display typeface with two styles: Regular and Alternates. The alternates extend the irregularity, offering variation to avoid mechanical repetition across settings. Over 270 glyphs are included with multi-lingual character support.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Serial as taking direct influence from the erratic handwriting found in letters written by the Son of Sam. They characterise the font as unpredictable and messy, reflecting its author, and note it is an uppercase-only family with Regular and Alternates styles, multi-lingual support and over 270 glyphs.

name: Letters Home
classification_notes: Letters Home is a casual handwritten script with extensive ligature support, mimicking natural pen-written correspondence rather than formal calligraphic lettering.
summary: A casual handwritten script with over 150 ligatures designed to mimic natural pen-written correspondence with an old-fashioned warmth.
description: Drawn with a thick pen and packed with more than 150 handwritten ligatures, the font aims to replicate the irregular, lived-in quality of genuine handwriting. The old-timey character makes it well suited to greeting cards, recipe cards, personal stationery and nostalgic branding. Its charm lies in the variation ligatures introduce, reducing the repetitive look common to many script fonts.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Letters Home as realistic and charming, with an old-timey feel akin to real handwriting. Over 150 handwritten ligatures transform text into a beautiful script scribed with a thick pen, warm and fuzzy like grandma's recipe or a love note home.

name: Stolen Hand
classification_notes: An all-caps marker-drawn typeface, hand-lettered with a Sharpie and digitised. Classified as script/handwritten due to its organic, hand-drawn character with natural marker edges.
summary: An all-caps marker font drawn with a Sharpie, digitised with natural edges and stylistic alternates for A-Z and 0-9.
description: Drawn by hand with a Sharpie and converted directly into a font, retaining the organic edges and weight variation of a real marker pen. The family is strictly uppercase and includes two sets of stylistic alternates, labelled Quirky and Sketches, covering the full A-Z and numerals. Best suited to casual display use where a hand-lettered, street-adjacent feel is needed.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Stolen Hand as a cool and casual handwritten font that is 100% uppercase and 100% marker. Drawn with a Sharpie and converted into a font, it features natural edges and realistic shapes, and comes with bonus stylistic alternates across two sets, Quirky and Sketches, covering A-Z and 0-9.

name: Lore
classification_notes: An all-caps handwriting font digitised directly from the designer's own handwriting, with deliberate irregularity and misalignment that places it firmly in the casual handwriting script category.
summary: An all-caps handwriting font traced from the designer's own script, with deliberate misalignment and imperfection across two weights.
description: Built from the designer's actual handwriting rather than a polished approximation, Lore carries the idiosyncratic irregularity of a real pen on paper. It is all-caps only, intentionally misaligned, and comes in regular and bold weights totalling over 450 glyphs. The roughness is the point, making it suited to informal display work where manufactured neatness would feel out of place.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Lore as a 'less than perfect' all-caps handwriting font made from the designer's own real handwriting. It is intentionally imperfect and misaligned, with over 450 glyphs available across regular and bold weights in two font styles.

name: Poppy Fineliner
classification_notes: A casual pen handwriting font with multiple letter alternates and hand-drawn doodle extras, placing it firmly in the informal script/handwriting category.
summary: A casual pen handwriting font with two alternates per character and a set of over 100 hand-drawn doodle illustrations included.
description: Built to mimic natural pen handwriting, Poppy Fineliner includes two versions of each letter and number so repetition can be avoided by mixing alternates. The font ships with a collection of over 100 pen-sketched doodles, making it useful for projects that need hand-drawn decorative elements alongside the lettering. Suited to greeting cards, social media graphics and informal branding.
foundry_blurb: Typeheist describe Poppy Fineliner as a cute and casual pen handwriting font, offering two versions of each letter and number for mixing and matching. It also includes over 100 pen sketches as hand-drawn doodles that can be added easily to designs.
